Name: training-free_layer-skipping_via_jacobian_norm
Title: Training-Free Layer-Skipping via Jacobian Norm for Efficient Inference
Short Hypothesis: The Jacobian norm of hidden state transformations serves as a reliable proxy for layer importance, enabling training-free layer-skipping decisions that preserve model accuracy while reducing inference latency.
Related Work: Existing layer-skipping methods often rely on training-based importance scores or heuristic thresholds, which introduce computational overhead or fail to adapt to dynamic input distributions. Recent work in training-free optimization (e.g., Spectral Quantization Routing, Geometric Signal Separation) leverages geometric properties of activations but does not directly address layer-skipping. Our proposal distinguishes itself by using the Jacobian norm, a computationally lightweight and theoretically grounded metric, to dynamically skip layers based on their contribution to the final output.
Abstract: Layer-skipping is a promising technique for reducing the computational cost of deep neural network inference. However, existing methods often require costly training or fail to adapt to dynamic input distributions. We propose a training-free layer-skipping framework that uses the Jacobian norm of hidden state transformations as a proxy for layer importance. By computing the Jacobian norm on-the-fly, we dynamically skip layers whose transformations contribute minimally to the final output, ensuring strict latency budgets while preserving model accuracy. We theoretically ground our approach in PAC-Bayes bounds, linking Jacobian norm thresholds to generalization stability under layer perturbation. Experiments on image classification and natural language processing tasks demonstrate that our method achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to existing layer-skipping techniques.

Experiments.name: Jacobian Norm Computation
Experiments.description: Compute the Jacobian norm of hidden state transformations for each layer during inference.

Experiments.name: Layer-Skipping Decision
Experiments.description: Dynamically skip layers based on a threshold derived from the Jacobian norm.

Experiments.name: Accuracy Evaluation
Experiments.description: Evaluate model accuracy on standard benchmarks after applying layer-skipping.

Experiments.name: Latency-Accuracy Tradeoff
Experiments.description: Compare the throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier of our method against existing layer-skipping techniques.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The Jacobian norm computation may introduce overhead for layers with large hidden states.
The method assumes that the Jacobian norm is a reliable proxy for layer importance, which may not hold for all architectures or tasks.
Dynamic layer-skipping decisions may lead to unpredictable latency fluctuations in real-time applications.
